title: LAMP Meat identification
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e8933","text":"Identifying species by identifying unique DNA sequences is common practice. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3rk4j","text":"In this case we will identify the presence or absence of pork DNA in a meat sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3hmh","text":"We will use the alkaline method for DNA extraction and LAMP method for DNA amplification and detection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9bog7","text":"This is not a new protocol but rather the detailing of a previously published protocol listed in the manuscript citation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"a9jo3","text":"Use appropriate PPE,  follow Safe Work Practices and follow local regulation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49o18","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"aok62","text":"Labware: minimum 2 1.5 mL PCR tubes, 2 x 0.1 mL PCR tubes with flat caps, commonly known as low-profile PCR tubes that match the device well size.
Devices:, LAMP/PCR/Water bath and thermometer, scale, 1- 10 μl micropipette, 100- 1000 μl micropipette and tips, centrifuge, Optional: vortex,  scale and boat.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":147,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9l6or","text":"
Reagents: Primers, LAMP master-mix, DNase free water, aliquot of meat to be tested in a prepared form
Others: crushed ice and tray, nitrile gloves, pipette rack , tube rack, labeling stain	","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eck5i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"56jb6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dn9vs","text":"Total:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"32ukl","text":"1 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fq2jj","text":"4 x 1.5 ml3 100 uL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7clra","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Use the 10X F3-B3 stock to make the final 0.2μM μM F3-B3 concentration at 25 μ L. 2uM*iv=0.2 μ M*25uL iv=0.2*25/2=2.5 μ L so add 2.5 μl of 10x F3-B3 10x stock to each Target and NTC tube .
Add DNase free water to 25 μL to each target and NTC tube . That is 4ul for Target tube and 4ul to NTC tube.

On ice tray we have 1X RT-LAMP Master Mix SuperScript IV ( https://www.fishersci.ca/shop/products/superscript-iv-rt-lamp-master-mix-3/p-9568005 - 1250 uL ) . The final volume should be 25μl and initial concentration of Master Mix stock is 2X. RT-LAMP Master Mix SuperScript IV 2x*iv=1x*25 μ l iv=1*25/2=12.5 μ L so add 25uL*(1X/2X)=12.5 μl from RT-LAMP Master Mix SuperScript IV stock to each target and NTC tube.
Take a 100 μL tube and label it and label it NTC (no template negative control).
Centrifuge the 4 lyophilized primer tubes 1 minute at 1000 G to move the powder to bottom as per IDT guide

Add 264 μL DNase free water to F3 tube , 242 μl to B3 tube 277 μl to FIP tube 271 μl to BIP tube Note: water can be substituted with 10 mM Tris pH8.0 (25 Celsius). Note: the quantities for 100 μM were specified by IDP in the "oligonucleotide specification sheet" shipped with the IDT product. Oligonucleotide specification sheet excerpt by IDT They can also be obtained by multiplying nmoles by 10 as in idt guide .

Label several (for instance 2) new 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes 10 μl-50 μM F3B3 and add in each 5 μl F3@100μM and 5 μl B3@100μM aliquots to a total of 10uL. Since the volume is double (5+5) the concentration of F3 is half and also the concentration for B3 is half. We do this to minimize freeze thaw cycle impact.

Load 8 x100 μL tubes with targets and negative control in the LAMP device and perform the LAMP protocol as specified by manufacturer as in https://assets.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets/BID/Application-Notes/superscript-iv-rt-lamp-master-mix-detect-dna-app-note.pdf set the machine to detection method: primers- isotermal amplification, 25ul quantity
Add 1 μL template DNA to targets tubes and 1 μL DNase free water to NTC tube.
Incubate the primer tubes at room temperature for at least 20 minutes following the addition of the water to allow for complete hydration of the DNA.
An orange color indicates no amplification, whereas a change from orange toyellow-green demonstrates the presence of LAMP amplicons.
Heat at 65 Celsius for 30 minutes . Note: that would be for fast reaction and this is a normal reaction so maybe 1 minute.
Inactivate at 95 Celsius for 2 minutes .
vortex briefly to mix all incoming - primer, superscript LAMP Master Mix

On ice tray prepare SYTO 9 stain to 5 μM final concentration. Note: It comes as 5 mM. Superscript4 Invitrogen kit came with 30 μL of 5000 μM SYTO 9 stain as stated on the tube ic*iv=fc*fv 5000uM*iv uL=5 uM*25 uL iv=5*25/5000=25/1000=0.025 uL. That is too little to accurately pipette so we need to dilute the initial stock to 10X concentration stock.

Prepare SYTO 9 stain 10x stock @ 50 μ M Centrifuge the SYTO 9 tube for 1 min at 1000G to gather the stain at the bottom. Label a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube to 10X SYTO 9 stock. Aliquot 2 μl SYTO 9 in a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube. ic*iv=fc*fv 5000 μ M*2 μ L=50 μ M*fv so fv=5000*2/50=200 μL. Since we start with 2 μ l we just need to add 198 μ l water. Add 198 μl DNase free water to the 10X SYTO 9 stock to a total of 200 μl Store at -15 and protect from light
To add SYTO 9 stain to a final concentration of 5 μM we add 5uM/500uM*25 μL=2.5 μL from the newly prepared 10x SYTO9 stock Add 2.5 μL 10X SYTO 9 stock to each targets and NTC tube.
Prepare ice tray for the reaction. On ice, thaw all components ( dye, Superscript LAMP master mix and template) to be used.

Make 10x of combined F3 and B3 primer stock. The final reaction requires 0.2μM F3 so a 10x stock should be 2 μM. The same for B3. For F3 the initial concentration(ic) is 50 μ M and the initial volume(iv) of the aliquot is 10 μ L. ic*iv=fc*fv so fv=(50 μM*10 μ L)/2 μM=250 μ L. Same for B3. Add 250-10= 240 μl of DNase free water to the 10 μl@50 μM F3B3 tube and re-label the tube 10x F3B3 primer tube. Note: some source recommends premixing the all primers in one 10x stock but we do only 2 pairs. Note: Additional explanation of similar calculations at https://www.optigene.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2012/06/OptiGene-LAMP-User-Guide-Assay-Design-Primers-1.pdf

Make 10x of combined FIP and BIP primer stock. The final concentration for FIP is 1.6 μM ( 8 times of the F3-B3!!!) so a 10x stock should be 16 μM. Same for BIP. The initial concentration is 50 μ M and the initial volume of the aliquot is 10 μ L. ic*iv=fc*fv so fv=(50μM*10μL)/16 μ M=31.25 μ L. Add 31.25-10= 21.25 μl of DNase free water to the 10 μl@50 μM FIP-BIP tube and re-label the tube 10x FIP-BIP primer tube.
Store all the 100 μM and all the 10 μL- 50 μM combined primer tubes at -15 Celsius
Relabel the original tubes to 100 μM F3, 100 μM B3, 100 μM FIP and 100 μM BIP.
Take a 100 μL tube and label it Target.

Label several (for instance 2) new 1.5 mL centrifuge tubes 10 μl-50 μM FIP-BIP and add in each 5 μl FIP@100μM and 5 μl BIP@100μM aliquots to a total of 1 μL. We do this to minimize freeze thaw cycle impact.
Use the 10X FIP-BIP stock to make the final 1.6 μM FIP-BIP concentration at 25uL. 16uM*iv=1.6 μ M*25uL iv=1.6*25/16=2.5 μ L so add 2.5 μl of 10x FIP-BIP 10x stock to each Target and NTC tube .
Mix all by gently reversing the 8 x100 μL tubes containing primers+syto9+DNA template+ water+ RT-LAMP mix
Quick spin at low speed in centrifuge to eliminate air bubbles
creator: openbioscience Adrian
